title: einsum GPU: support mixed repeated/free indices (e.g. 'iij->j')
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Bugfix Batch 6 / Dispatch A2 (#821) closed the homogeneous repeated-index cases ('ii->', 'ii->i', 'ii', 'iii->') via on-device diagonal extraction (as_strided shape [N], stride [1+N+N^2+...]). Mixed cases like 'iij->j' (where i repeats but j is a free index) currently surface Err(NotImplementedOnCuda { op: 'einsum_repeated_index_mixed' }). The decomposition needs to (1) extract a 2D 'i' diagonal under each j independently and (2) keep j on-device — which existing strided_copy plus sum_dim can do but only after we add per-axis diagonal indexing logic to einsum_single_repeated_gpu. PyTorch supports these on CUDA so this is a parity gap, but a smaller surface than the trace/diagonal case the parent dispatch closed.
